title: Chile: COVID-19 weekly death rate by vaccination status
subtitle: Death rates are calculated as the number of deaths in each group, divided by the total number of people in this group. This is given per 100,000 people.
caption:
Chile: COVID-19 weekly death rate by vaccination status, All ages
Death rates are calculated as the number of deaths in each group, divided by the total number of people in this group. This is given per 100,000 people.

This line chart tracks the weekly death rate from COVID-19 in Chile across four vaccination categories from October 2021 through March 2023, with the vertical axis measuring deaths per 100,000 people. The mortality rate for the 'All ages' group is age-standardized to account for the different vaccination rates of older and younger people. Four distinct lines represent the groups: 0 or 1 dose (red), 2 doses (brown), 3 doses (green), and 4 doses (blue). The data shows a massive spike in mortality during early 2022, where the lines for the unvaccinated and partially vaccinated populations surged well above the fully vaccinated groups. At the peak around March 2022, the death rate for those with 0 or 1 dose and those with 2 doses both climbed to nearly 12 deaths per 100,000 people. In contrast, the line for individuals with 3 doses reached a maximum of roughly 4 deaths per 100,000 during the same period, while the group with 4 doses remained below 2. Following this peak, all lines descended sharply, converging near the bottom of the chart by mid-2022. Through the remainder of the timeline, the rates fluctuated at low levels, generally staying between 0 and 3 deaths per 100,000. The visual gap between the red and brown lines versus the green and blue lines is widest during the early 2022 surge, narrowing significantly as the overall death rates fell in the subsequent months. The highest recorded values on the chart belong to the 0 or 1 dose and 2 dose groups during the early 2022 peak.
